This repository explores Machine Learning through projects, scripts, and tutorials. It covers data preprocessing, model training, evaluation, and deployment using frameworks like scikit-learn, TensorFlow, and PyTorch, with applications in classification, regression, NLP, and computer vision.

In competitive industries like telecoms, retaining customers is as vital as gaining them. Using real-world data, this churn research project analyzes why users leave and how to stop it. High-throughput machine learning pipeline designed to decode the regulatory logic of microexon splicing. The framework uses Support Vector Regression (SVR) to predict microexon inclusion levels (PSI) based on the expression profiles of Splicing Factors (SFs), utilizing SHAP values to identify the primary regulatory drivers.

flowMagic is an R package for automated bivariate gating of flow cytometry (FCM) data. It uses a machine-learning model trained on both experts data and a large, high-quality FCM dataset generated by EVE Online players. The package also provides an extensive visualization toolkit and integrates seamlessly with the flowCore/flowWorkspace frameworks

CircumSpectrum is a flux-based framework that converts bulk and single-cell RNA-seq data into metabolic fingerprints using genome-scale models and machine learning. It includes RADAR, SOURIS, and CORALIE modules for reaction-level, cross-cohort, and multi-tier correlation analyses.

An analytical implementation of Unsupervised Machine Learning to identify customer personas. This framework utilizes K-Means clustering and feature scaling to segment users based on behavioral data, optimized through WCSS and Elbow Method heuristics.

Collection of applied AI projects covering Python, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Transformers, Computer Vision, and NLP. Demonstrates practical system building including chatbots, recommendation engines, data analysis, and model training using modern AI tools and frameworks.

Heart disease is a leading cause of mortality, making early diagnosis crucial. Traditional methods are time-consuming and costly, but machine learning (ML) can improve accuracy and efficiency. ML models, such as Decision Trees, SVM, Random Forest, and Neural Networks, analyze patient data like age to predict heart disease risk.

This repository provides the numerical framework for solving ill-posed inverse problems using a regularization strategy based on the parametrization of the solution via a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP). Unlike typical machine learning approaches, this method does not require training data and interprets the MLP as an implicit neural representation.
